body_txt: A character's outfit, style of clothing or other cosmetic additions that are changed over the course of a series, often happens with a sequel or new season. This can apply to a superhero's costume, a military uniform, a character's Limited Wardrobe or even their Unlimited Wardrobe , the change with be different in both small and big ways. In most cases it will be a similar outfit but with a different color scheme, but may be something radically different but in the same theme. Such as a Princess Classic that switches their Pimped-Out Dress for another Pimped-Out Dress with different colors, fabrics, shapes or decorations. Or a Powered Armor just has a few small vents and fins added. Smaller changes are more likely when Clothes Make the Legend . The reasons for this is either an attempt to keep things fresh or trying to improve and update the visual design, making the old costume a form of So Last Season . It may also be an attempt to reflect cultural changes or new standards of style and versatility (which is one of the most common reasons for uniform updates in Real Life ). It may also be an attempt to providing new content in a Merchandise-Driven show. In live-action works, changes can be to make a costume more comfortable and easier to work with for the actors wearing them (so it's an Enforced Element to avoid actors risking physical harm, and may be due to actor complaints ). In computer animation and video games, increases in processing power often allows for greater detail than before, letting metal look like metal and cloth behave like cloth instead of an amorphous shape. The larger changes may give a justification for the change, while the smaller ones will just assume the audience will either not notice or accept that the costume was tweaked in the time between installments. Though even after an upgrade a previous outfit may become the Iconic Outfit . It is typically not commented on directly, though Lampshade Hanging can happen, but it's just a new Super-Trope to a large number of other tropes: May overlap with Visual Development if the change in outfit reflects a change in the character themself. Compare Good Costume Switch , Evil Costume Switch . See also Art Evolution , Adrenaline Makeover .
